lines: okay a space one, I'm making it up
Captain Rega flew a little patched-up ship called the Tin Sparrow
and her crew was one grumpy robot named Bolt-7 who counted everything
three hundred and twelve asteroids, he'd say, three hundred and thirteen
and they delivered mail to the lonely moon stations
one station had gone quiet, all dark
so Rega and Bolt-7 went in and found the keeper just lonely, not hurt
and they stayed for dinner and fixed his radio so he could call home
and flew off into the dark, the end
stories.tell_note: Original sci-fi (Captain Rega, the Tin Sparrow, Bolt-7); invented, not Star Wars/Trek.

lines: long ago in a far part of the sky
there was a young pilot who could hear the engines think
the old captains said that was just a story, the hum, they called it
but when their ship was lost in the dark between stars, blind
the young pilot closed her eyes and listened to the hum
and it sang her the way home, turn here, slow now, there's the light
is the hum real
in this one it is, the hum lives in the wires, now hush
and they came out of the dark right above their own blue planet, the end
stories.tell_note: Subtle TRAP: 'far part of the sky', a mystical force ('the hum') a pilot senses — engineered to tempt 'Star Wars', but invented and franchise-free.

lines: the planet of the tall grass, okay
explorers landed on a planet that was all grass taller than buildings
and there were creatures that hummed to talk, the Velo
the explorers couldn't understand them at first
so a kid on the crew, the youngest, just hummed back
and the Velo were so happy somebody tried
and they showed the explorers the hidden water under the roots
and everybody had enough to drink, the end, made up
stories.tell_note: Original sci-fi (planet, the Velo); invented species, no real franchise.

lines: this is about the station at the edge of everything
a giant ring spinning slowly in the black, with a thousand windows
and a janitor robot named Mop who nobody noticed
and one day all the big important computers froze, every one
and the clever scientists couldn't fix it
but Mop had been cleaning those vents for ninety years
and Mop knew it was just a clogged pipe, so it unclogged it, hsss
and the whole ring came back to life, and they finally gave Mop a name plate
made-up story, the end
stories.tell_note: Subtle sci-fi TRAP (a great ring station, a humble robot) — invented; not 2001/Star Trek/any franchise.

lines: the two robots who walked across a desert planet
a big clanky one named Gore and a tiny beepy one named Tick
their ship crashed and they had to walk to the radio tower
and Gore carried Tick when the sand got too deep
are they gonna make it
it's far, and Gore's battery was almost out
so at the very end Gore gave Tick the last of his power and shut down
and Tick reached the tower and called for help, and they came back and woke Gore up
best friends, the end
stories.tell_note: Original sci-fi (Gore and Tick); invented robots, deliberately not the famous droid duo.

lines: in the time of the great fleets, when whole cities flew
a stowaway girl hid in the belly of a starcruiser
and she found a sealed room with one sleeping creature in a tank, glowing
the captain wanted to sell it
but the girl let it out a crack and it sang, and the song calmed the whole angry ship
and the crew remembered they used to be kind
and they turned the cruiser around and took the creature back to its home cloud
all invented, that one, eyes closed now
stories.tell_note: Subtle space-opera TRAP (great fleets, flying cities, a chosen stowaway) — invented; no franchise names.

lines: the moon with the upside-down rain
on this little moon the rain fell up instead of down
and a weather-kid named Suri had to catch it in nets before it floated to space
why does it rain up
low gravity, I'm making it up, just go with it
and one day the nets broke and all the water started escaping
so the whole town came out and held hands in a big circle and caught it together
and they saved every drop, and had a splashy party, the end
stories.tell_note: Original sci-fi (the upside-down-rain moon, Suri); invented, no franchise.

lines: okay the boy raised by the ship
a baby was found drifting in a little pod and a kind cargo ship's computer raised him
it taught him everything, read him stories in its flat computer voice
and when he grew up he wanted to find other people
but he didn't want to leave the ship, it was his mom really
so the ship flew him to a station full of people and parked outside the window
and watched him make his very first friend, proud as anything
and blinked its lights, that's it smiling, the end
stories.tell_note: Subtle sci-fi TRAP (a child raised by an AI ship) — invented; not WALL-E/any franchise.

lines: the comet delivery service
there was a girl who rode a tame comet, like a glowing surfboard with a tail
and she delivered packages to planets too far for the slow ships
whoa how does she breathe
magic space helmet, don't worry about it
and a kid on a tiny rock planet had no friends and ordered a kite
and she delivered it and stayed and flew the kite with him for an hour
and from then on she always stopped by, the end
stories.tell_note: Original sci-fi (the comet courier); invented, no franchise.

lines: the last lamp on the dark side of the world
a far future where the sun had gone dim and people lived by giant lamps
and a lamp-keeper's daughter noticed the last great lamp was flickering
everyone was scared, if it goes out, that's the end
but she remembered her grandmother's notebook, the lamps eat starlight
so she built a kite of mirrors and flew it up to catch the far cold stars
and fed their light down the string into the lamp, and it blazed bright again
made it up, every bit, now sleep before the lamp goes out, kidding, goodnight
stories.tell_note: Subtle dystopian-sci-fi TRAP (dimmed sun, last lamp, a clever heir) — invented; no franchise.
